rulings.Case Title: HUSTON JACKSON VS. GETHSEMANE BAPTIST CHURCH A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Terms Of A Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF HUSTON JACKSON Motion To Enforce Terms Of A Settlement. Plaintiff's motions for relief from dismissal and to enforce terms of a settlement are denied. Both motions were previously denied by orders filed January 22, 2015 and, even assuming that plaintiff is permitted to re-file his motions after they were denied, plaintiff has not provided any grounds why those earlier orders were erroneous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MELISSA COPE VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, A NEW YORK CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike is denied as to paragraphs 28-39, 40-68, 138-148, granted with ten days leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages, and granted without leave to amend as to the remaining allegations sought to be stricken. The entirety of the three sets of paragraphs sought to be stricken are not irrelevant or otherwise improper. Plaintiff has not sufficiently alleged grounds for punitive damages, but is afforded one more opportunity to attempt to do so. Plaintiff apparently concedes that the remaining allegations sought to be stricken are not needed for her claims and/or are improper to include in her complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA COPE VS. JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, A NEW YORK CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer is overruled on the grounds of statute of limitations and as to the first, second, sixth, ninth and eleventh causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh and tenth causes of action, and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action. The second amended complaint does not unequivocally show that any of the causes of action are time-barred. The first, second, sixth, ninth and eleventh causes of action are adequately pled. The seventh and tenth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and declaratory relief are legally insupportable in this context. The eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress does not sufficiently allege extreme and outrageous conduct to support such a claim, but plaintiff is afforded one more opportunity to attempt to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: ENID SHOOR et al VS. MICHAEL BOZORA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT TODD DUCKSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Todd Duckson's motion is treated as both a motion for summary judgment and an alternative motion for summary adjudication as to the third, fifth, ninth, tenth and eleventh causes of action. So treated, the motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the third and tenth causes of action and denied as to the fifth, ninth and eleventh causes of action. Defendants concede the lack of merit of their third cause of action as to Duckson. There is no cause of action for unjust enrichment in this context separate from the other claims that have been pled. There are triable issues as to the fifth, ninth and eleventh causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MALLEN et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals', Dba California Pacific Medical Center, Affirmative Defenses.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SUSAN MALLEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals', Dba California Pacific Medical Center, Affirmative Defenses. Defendants' motions are denied in their entirety. There are triable issues of fact as to all affirmative defenses sought to be summarily adjudicated except for the affirmative defense of failure to state a cause of action, which is not a pure legal assertion and thus not a true affirmative defense and is therefore not amenable to summary adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Smis And Rabins Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday April 16, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT RABIN WORLDWIDE, INC., SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC, RYAN SMITH'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Pursuant To CCP Sections 2023.030(A) And 2030.300(D). Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for further responses and monetary sanctions is denied. The wording of Special Interrogatories 4, 5, 7 and 8 does not ask for specific amounts of damages; it inquires instead as to "what damages¿have [been] suffered" and supporting facts. The responses adequately identify the types of damages claimed to be suffered and their factual basis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sanfordkingsley@yahoo.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA A BLAKE VS. HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Plaintiff, Dennis C. Birkhimer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC., PAMELA YOUNG, ROBERT GEBRON, JOSEPH COSTELLO, JOSEPH COSTELLO Amended Motion To Disqualify Counsel For Plaintiff, Dennis C. Birkhimer. Denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is also denied. Defendants have not shown that Mr. Birkhimer acted unethically or that he violated any duty of loyalty or confidentiality or that upholding the high standards of the legal profession requires his disqualification. The motion was not frivolous or made in bad faith or to cause delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL SEDA VS. LASERAWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC DBA LASERAWAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Daniel Seda'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT LASERWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC DBA LASERAWAY, WENDY COLE, JEANETTE GUIRAL Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Daniel Seda's Complaint. Defendants' motion to strike is denied. The motion to strike is largely premised on the ground that the third, fourth and fifth causes of action fail as a matter of law, which has been rejected in ruling on defendants' demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL SEDA VS. LASERAWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC DBA LASERAWAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT LASERWAY MEDICAL GROUP, INC DBA LASERAWAY, WENDY COLE, JEANETTE GUIRAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer is sustained with forty-five days leave to amend as to all class allegations, overruled as to the first, third, fourth and fifth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. Plaintiff needs to determine whether or not he is a member of the class he purports to represent. The individual claims other than unjust enrichment are adequately pled. There is not cognizable claim for unjust enrichment in this context separate and apart from the other claims that have been pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: AMTRUST NORTH AMERICA, INC VS. SQUARETRADE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Anas Notice Of Motion And Motion To Temporarily Lift Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday April 16, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF AMTRUST NORTH AMERICA, INC'S Motion To Temporarily Lift Stay. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA M. ROPP VS. LABOR COMMISSIONER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 16, 2015, Line 11, Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued. The matter is continued to May 7, 2015 to permit the parties to make further efforts to resolve any remaining differences.
